<Routes>
  <Route path='/dashboard' element={<DashboardLayout />}>
    <Route index element={<DashboardHome />} />
    <Route path='users' element={<Users />} />

</Routes>

v3_senior.code:
const DashboardHome = lazy(() => import('./DashboardHome'));
const Users = lazy(() => import('./Users'));

<Routes>
  <Route path='/dashboard' element={<DashboardLayout />}>
    <Route index element={<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}><DashboardHome /></Suspense>} />
    <Route path='users' element={<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}><Users /></Suspense>} />
  </Route>
</Routes>

v5_principal.improvements_over_v4: Parallel data + streaming with Suspense + Await
v5_principal.what_makes_it_principal: Routes load data in parallel; UI streams as data arrives
v5_principal.what_team_learns_from_this: Routing is data fetching layer; parallel by default

when_to_use_which_version: V1: never. V2-V3: typical. V4: modern. V5: data-heavy apps.
